post.summary:
Clear deletes exactly the records captured by the Export/Transfer run that produced the
manifest. Memories recalled (or re-created) since the capture survive, as do events that
still have memories.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description:
Clear deletes exactly the records captured by the Export/Transfer run that produced the
manifest: memories are deleted only if neither created nor recalled since the snapshot, and
events only once they have no memories left. Manifests are held in memory - a restart discards
them.

get.summary:
GetEvents lists events filtered by time range, significance range, and group, paginated by
limit/offset (see GetEventsRequest). significance_extremum swaps the significance_min/max
range for "only events tied at the highest (or lowest) significance value" instead.

parameters.name: timeStartMin
parameters.description: UnixNano inclusive lower bound on time_start; 0 (the default) means no lower bound

parameters.name: timeStartMax
parameters.description: UnixNano inclusive upper bound on time_start; 0 (the default) means no upper bound

parameters.name: timeEndMin
parameters.description: UnixNano inclusive lower bound on time_end; 0 (the default) means no lower bound

parameters.name: timeEndMax
parameters.description: UnixNano inclusive upper bound on time_end; 0 (the default) means no upper bound

parameters.name: significanceMin
parameters.description: inclusive lower bound on significance; 0 (the default) means no bound, NOT a literal significance-of-zero filter

parameters.name: significanceMax
parameters.description: inclusive upper bound on significance; 0 (the default) means no bound, NOT a literal significance-of-zero filter

parameters.name: memories
parameters.description: when true, include each matching event's memories in the response

parameters.name: group
parameters.description: optional: restrict to events carrying this group label

parameters.name: orderBy
parameters.description: "significance" (significance desc, then time desc) or "timestamp" (time desc); defaults to significance

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: page size; a value <= 0 selects the server default (25); values above the server cap (200) are silently clamped down to it

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: rows to skip for pagination; negative values are clamped to 0; no upper bound

parameters.name: significanceExtremum
parameters.description:
when set, ignore significance_min/significance_max and return only events tied at that significance value among the other filters (time range, group) - not a range; 0 (default) applies no extremum

 - SIGNIFICANCE_EXTREMUM_UNSPECIFIED: no extremum filtering; significance_min/significance_max (if set) apply as a normal range
 - SIGNIFICANCE_EXTREMUM_HIGHEST: return only events at the highest significance value among those matching the other filters
 - SIGNIFICANCE_EXTREMUM_LOWEST: return only events at the lowest significance value among those matching the other filters

post.summary:
Events
StoreEvent creates an event, optionally with nested memories (each defaulted to the new
event's id when unset). An event below event.minimumSignificance is quietly dropped - see
StoreEventResponse.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description:
Event is a named time span with its own significance, optionally carrying relationships to
other events and/or nested memories. memories/relationships are populated on read only when
requested (GetEventById, GetEvents); on StoreEvent nested memories are write-only input, each
stored via the same rules as StoreMemory.

post.summary:
MergeEvents re-points every memory of merge_from onto merge_to. merge_to must already exist;
merge_from need not - an absent one simply matches no memories.

Summarization
ReplaceMemoriesWithSummary deletes every memory of an event and replaces them with a single
caller-supplied summary memory, in one transaction; see ReplaceMemoriesWithSummaryRequest.

get.summary:
GetEventById fetches a single event by id; set memories to also load its nested memories.
NotFound if the event does not exist.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: the event to fetch

parameters.name: memories
parameters.description: true also loads the event's memories into the response

delete.summary:
DeleteEvent deletes an event. When memories is true its memories are deleted too; otherwise
they are kept but detached (their event_id is cleared). NotFound if the event does not exist.

parameters.name: memories
parameters.description: true also deletes the event's memories; false only detaches them

post.summary:
EndEvent sets an event's time_end (defaults to now when unset). NotFound if the event does
not exist.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: the event to end

patch.summary:
UpdateEventSignificance changes an event's significance, either as an absolute value or via
placement (see SignificancePlacement). NotFound if the event does not exist.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: the event to update

post.summary:
Transfer and archive (embedded -> centralised)
Export snapshots the whole store into an archive object in S3 and records a manifest of
exactly what was captured; with clear set, the captured records are deleted once the upload
has succeeded.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description:
Export snapshots every event and memory into an archive object in S3 and records a manifest of
exactly what was captured; with clear set, the captured records are deleted after a successful
upload (writes and recalls landing mid-run survive, exactly as Clear does).

post.summary:
Import streams an archive object from S3 into the store, upserting every record by id with
its full state preserved. Idempotent; aborts on the first error.

ImportBatch upserts full-state rows by id directly - the ingest half of Transfer. Nothing is
defaulted or gated on minimum significance; the rows carry their history.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description:
ImportBatchRequest carries full-state rows (original timestamps, recall state, significance,
group, event links) to be upserted by id into the store as-is - a data migration, not a fresh
write. It is the ingest half of Transfer and is re-sendable: re-importing the same rows is
idempotent.

get.summary:
GetMemories lists memories filtered by time range, significance range, and group, paginated
by limit/offset (see GetMemoriesRequest).

parameters.name: timestampMin
parameters.description: UnixNano inclusive lower bound on time_stamp; 0 (the default) means no lower bound

parameters.name: timestampMax
parameters.description: UnixNano inclusive upper bound on time_stamp; 0 (the default) means no upper bound

parameters.name: significanceMin
parameters.description: inclusive lower bound on significance; 0 (the default) means no bound, NOT a literal significance-of-zero filter

parameters.name: significanceMax
parameters.description: inclusive upper bound on significance; 0 (the default) means no bound, NOT a literal significance-of-zero filter

parameters.name: group
parameters.description: optional: restrict to memories carrying this group label

parameters.name: orderBy
parameters.description: "significance" (significance desc, then time desc) or "timestamp" (time desc); defaults to significance

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: page size; a value <= 0 selects the server default (25); values above the server cap (200) are silently clamped down to it

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: rows to skip for pagination; negative values are clamped to 0; no upper bound

post.summary:
Memories
StoreMemory creates a memory. A memory below memory.minimumSignificance is quietly dropped -
see StoreMemoryResponse.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description:
Memory is a blob, opaque to the server, with a significance and timestamp, optionally linked to
an event. Recalling it (RecallMemories) reinforces it: the decay clock resets and its effective
significance rises. is_binary controls whether the body is indexed for content search (see
SearchMemories) - a binary body is never indexed.

post.summary: DeleteMemories deletes the given memories by id; unknown ids are silently ignored.

RecallMemories returns the requested memories and reinforces each one: its recall time is
reset to now (resetting its decay clock) and its recall count is incremented (raising its
effective significance during consolidation).

SearchMemories finds memories via the optional content-search index; see
SearchMemoriesRequest.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description:
SearchMemoriesRequest queries the optional secondary content-search index (opensearch.enabled)
for memories whose body matches the query. Results are always re-read from the primary store,
which remains authoritative; ids the index returns that the primary store no longer holds are
silently dropped. When reinforce is true the matched memories are recalled - resetting their
decay clocks and raising their effective significance - rather than merely fetched.

patch.summary:
UpdateMemory applies a partial update to an existing memory: only the fields carrying a value
(significance, body, event_id, group, time_stamp) overwrite the stored row. is_binary and
is_summary are NOT updatable here - they are set at creation and by ReplaceMemoriesWithSummary
- so any updated content must stay consistent with the memory's existing is_binary/is_summary:
a non-binary memory is re-indexed for content search, so its new body must remain valid text,
and a summary memory should keep summary-appropriate content. An unknown id returns NotFound
(no memory is created).

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: auto-generated UUID if omitted on create; max 128 characters

post.summary:
Purge deletes every event and memory in the store. While it runs, all other Hippocampus RPCs
are rejected with Unavailable.

Sleep manually triggers a consolidation cycle out of schedule and resets the periodic timer.
Rejected with FailedPrecondition on a read/write replica (consolidation.enabled: false),
which must never run its own cycle.

get.summary:
GetSummarizationCandidates returns events identified by the most recent sleep cycle as having
accumulated enough quiet, unsummarized memories to be worth condensing via
ReplaceMemoriesWithSummary. A point-in-time snapshot, refreshed only when
consolidation.summarizationMinMemories is configured.

post.summary:
Transfer streams the whole store directly into a centralised instance's ImportBatch RPC,
recording a manifest like Export; with clear set the captured records are deleted once the
target has accepted every batch.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description:
Transfer streams every event and memory directly to a centralised instance's ImportBatch RPC
(address from transfer.targetAddress); like Export it records a manifest, and with clear set
deletes the captured records once the target has accepted them all.

timeEnd.title: UnixNano; 0 (the default) uses the current server time

significance.title: the new absolute significance; must be >= 1 to take effect. IMPORTANT: 0 with no placement is a no-op - it leaves the event's existing significance unchanged; there is no way to reset an event to unranked via this RPC

placement.title: write-only: rank relative to existing values (see SignificancePlacement); takes precedence over significance when set

timeStamp.title: UnixNano; defaults to the current time when unset (0) on create; required (> 0) once defaulted

significance.title: 0 = unranked (rank it later via UpdateMemory/placement); must be >= 0; higher ranks more significant. On UpdateMemory, 0 with no placement leaves the existing significance unchanged - it cannot reset a memory to unranked

eventId.title: optional: leave unset to store the memory unassociated with any event

body.title: required (non-empty) on create; a proto3 string, so always valid UTF-8 on the wire - encode true binary payloads (e.g. base64) before setting is_binary; optionally capped by memory.limit.sizeBytes (0 = unlimited)

isBinary.title: set once at creation; not updatable via UpdateMemory. TRUE bodies are opaque and never indexed for content search (see SearchMemories)

timeRecalled.title: read-only: UnixNano of the most recent recall (RecallMemories, or SearchMemories with reinforce); 0 if never recalled; ignored on write

recallCount.title: read-only: number of times the memory has been recalled; ignored on write

isSummary.title: read-only: true for the summary memory created by ReplaceMemoriesWithSummary; not updatable via UpdateMemory

group.title: optional freeform grouping/context label (system, subsystem, owner, ...); max 128 characters

placement.title: write-only: rank relative to existing values (see SignificancePlacement)

HippocampusUpdateMemoryBody.description:
Memory is a blob, opaque to the server, with a significance and timestamp, optionally linked to
an event. Recalling it (RecallMemories) reinforces it: the decay clock resets and its effective
significance rises. is_binary controls whether the body is indexed for content search (see
SearchMemories) - a binary body is never indexed.

SignificancePlacementMode.title:
- UNSPECIFIED: ignore placement; use the absolute significance value
 - ABOVE: rank just above the anchor, below the next-higher value
 - BELOW: rank just below the anchor, above the next-lower value
 - BETWEEN: rank strictly between the anchor (lower) and upper bounds

protoBool.description:
Bool is a tri-state boolean (rather than proto3's inherent bool) so a caller can distinguish an
explicitly-set value from one left absent. Used for Memory.is_binary; UNSPECIFIED and FALSE are
currently treated identically by the server (both mean not-binary) - only TRUE marks a memory
as binary.

 - UNSPECIFIED: not set; the server currently treats this the same as FALSE
 - FALSE: explicitly not binary
 - TRUE: binary; the body is opaque and never indexed for content search

protoClearRequest.description:
Clear deletes exactly the records captured by the Export/Transfer run that produced the
manifest: memories are deleted only if neither created nor recalled since the snapshot, and
events only once they have no memories left. Manifests are held in memory - a restart discards
them.

id.title: auto-generated UUID if omitted on create; max 128 characters

timeStart.title: UnixNano; defaults to the current time when unset (0) on create; required (> 0) once defaulted

timeEnd.title: UnixNano; 0 means the event has not ended yet (see EndEvent); when set, must not be before time_start

significance.title: 0 = unranked (rank it later via UpdateEventSignificance/placement); must be >= 0; higher ranks more significant

memoriesConsolidated.title: read-only: true once a sleep cycle has deleted (consolidated) some of this event's memories; ignored on write

relationshipSignificance.title: read-only: sum of relationships' significance values, calculated by the server; ignored on write

relationships.title: relationships to other events, weighted into this event's memories' value (see consolidation.relationshipSignificanceWeight)

memories.title: populated on read only when requested (GetEventById/GetEvents); write-only input on StoreEvent, stored via the same rules as StoreMemory

group.title: optional freeform grouping/context label (system, subsystem, owner, ...); max 128 characters

placement.title: write-only: rank relative to existing values (see SignificancePlacement)

protoEvent.description:
Event is a named time span with its own significance, optionally carrying relationships to
other events and/or nested memories. memories/relationships are populated on read only when
requested (GetEventById, GetEvents); on StoreEvent nested memories are write-only input, each
stored via the same rules as StoreMemory.

protoExportRequest.description:
Export snapshots every event and memory into an archive object in S3 and records a manifest of
exactly what was captured; with clear set, the captured records are deleted after a successful
upload (writes and recalls landing mid-run survive, exactly as Clear does).

totalCount.title: events matching the filter, ignoring limit/offset — for pagination
memories matching the filter, ignoring limit/offset — for pagination

protoImportBatchRequest.description:
ImportBatchRequest carries full-state rows (original timestamps, recall state, significance,
group, event links) to be upserted by id into the store as-is - a data migration, not a fresh
write. It is the ingest half of Transfer and is re-sendable: re-importing the same rows is
idempotent.

id.title: auto-generated UUID if omitted on create; max 128 characters

timeStamp.title: UnixNano; defaults to the current time when unset (0) on create; required (> 0) once defaulted

significance.title: 0 = unranked (rank it later via UpdateMemory/placement); must be >= 0; higher ranks more significant. On UpdateMemory, 0 with no placement leaves the existing significance unchanged - it cannot reset a memory to unranked

eventId.title: optional: leave unset to store the memory unassociated with any event

body.title: required (non-empty) on create; a proto3 string, so always valid UTF-8 on the wire - encode true binary payloads (e.g. base64) before setting is_binary; optionally capped by memory.limit.sizeBytes (0 = unlimited)

isBinary.title: set once at creation; not updatable via UpdateMemory. TRUE bodies are opaque and never indexed for content search (see SearchMemories)

timeRecalled.title: read-only: UnixNano of the most recent recall (RecallMemories, or SearchMemories with reinforce); 0 if never recalled; ignored on write

recallCount.title: read-only: number of times the memory has been recalled; ignored on write

isSummary.title: read-only: true for the summary memory created by ReplaceMemoriesWithSummary; not updatable via UpdateMemory

group.title: optional freeform grouping/context label (system, subsystem, owner, ...); max 128 characters

placement.title: write-only: rank relative to existing values (see SignificancePlacement)

protoMemory.description:
Memory is a blob, opaque to the server, with a significance and timestamp, optionally linked to
an event. Recalling it (RecallMemories) reinforces it: the decay clock resets and its effective
significance rises. is_binary controls whether the body is indexed for content search (see
SearchMemories) - a binary body is never indexed.

mergeTo.title: the surviving event; must already exist

mergeFrom.title: the event whose memories are moved to merge_to; need not exist - an absent one simply matches no memories

eventId.title: the related event's id; not validated against existing events

significance.title: this relationship's weight; unvalidated (may be negative or zero) - not part of the significance registry, never ranked

protoRelationship.description:
Relationship is a directed reference from one event to another, carrying its own significance,
which is weighted into the value calculation of the referencing event's memories (see
consolidation.relationshipSignificanceWeight).

limit.title: maximum results; a value <= 0 selects the default (10)

eventId.title: optional: restrict matches to a single event

reinforce.title: route matches through recall, reinforcing them

group.title: optional: restrict matches to memories carrying this group label

protoSearchMemoriesRequest.description:
SearchMemoriesRequest queries the optional secondary content-search index (opensearch.enabled)
for memories whose body matches the query. Results are always re-read from the primary store,
which remains authoritative; ids the index returns that the primary store no longer holds are
silently dropped. When reinforce is true the matched memories are recalled - resetting their
decay clocks and raising their effective significance - rather than merely fetched.

protoSignificanceExtremum.description:
SignificanceExtremum selects "all events tied at the highest (or lowest) significance value"
instead of a min/max range - the tied set is computed dynamically from whichever events match
GetEventsRequest's other filters (time range, group), not a fixed value the caller supplies.
Mutually exclusive with significance_min/significance_max (InvalidArgument if both are set).

 - SIGNIFICANCE_EXTREMUM_UNSPECIFIED: no extremum filtering; significance_min/significance_max (if set) apply as a normal range
 - SIGNIFICANCE_EXTREMUM_HIGHEST: return only events at the highest significance value among those matching the other filters
 - SIGNIFICANCE_EXTREMUM_LOWEST: return only events at the lowest significance value among those matching the other filters

anchor.title: anchor significance value; must be >= 1 unless anchor_id is set

anchorId.title: anchor by an existing item's id, whose significance is looked up; that item must already be ranked (significance > 0)

upper.title: BETWEEN only: the upper bound significance; must be >= 1 (and greater than anchor) unless upper_id is set

upperId.title: BETWEEN only: the upper bound by an existing item's id; that item must already be ranked (significance > 0)

protoSignificancePlacement.description:
SignificancePlacement positions an item's significance relative to existing values instead of
taking the absolute `significance` field. It exists because significance is a dense integer with
no room between adjacent values: this lets a client say "just above the 5s" or "between 5 and 6"
and have the server open a gap for it. Write-only on StoreEvent/StoreMemory/UpdateMemory and
UpdateEventSignificance; it is never populated on read responses. When mode is UNSPECIFIED the
absolute `significance` value is used as-is (0 meaning unranked).

id.title: the stored event's id; empty when the event was not retained

memoryCount.title: nested memories retained (best-effort; those rejected/dropped are not reported individually)

rejected.title: true when the event was dropped for significance below event.minimumSignificance

protoStoreEventResponse.description:
StoreEventResponse carries the stored event's id and how many of its nested memories were
retained. An event whose significance is below event.minimumSignificance is quietly forgotten,
like a brain that simply does not retain the insignificant: no error is returned, id is empty,
memory_count is 0 (its nested memories are not stored either), and rejected is true.

Nested-memory creation is best-effort: the event is committed first, so a nested memory that
fails validation, is dropped for insignificance, or hits a store error does not fail the event
create and is not individually reported here - memory_count is only the count actually retained.
Store memories individually if you need per-memory success/failure.

id.title: the stored memory's id; empty when the memory was not retained

rejected.title: true when the memory was dropped for significance below memory.minimumSignificance

protoStoreMemoryResponse.description:
StoreMemoryResponse carries the stored memory's id. A memory whose significance is below
memory.minimumSignificance is quietly forgotten, like a brain that simply does not retain the
insignificant: no error is returned, id is empty, and rejected is true.

protoSummarizationCandidate.description:
SummarizationCandidate identifies an event whose memories have accumulated enough, and gone
quiet for long enough, to be worth condensing into a single summary memory via
ReplaceMemoriesWithSummary.

protoTransferRequest.description:
Transfer streams every event and memory directly to a centralised instance's ImportBatch RPC
(address from transfer.targetAddress); like Export it records a manifest, and with clear set
deletes the captured records once the target has accepted them all.
